name: Chinese Steamed Cracked Crabs
recipeIngredient: 1 Crab, live in the shell, about 2 pounds
2 Green onions, bruised and cut in half
1 tbsp Shao Hsing wine or dry sherry
Ginger, Green Onion and Vinegar Dipping Sauce
2 tbsp Finely shredded ginger, preferably young ginger
recipeInstructions:
1. It's crab season here in the San Francisco Bay Area. Heap and heaps of luscious Dungeness crabs are showing up all over the place. Steamed or live, take your pick! I was in Oakland's Chinatown last weekend shopping at the Sam Yick grocery and saw a couple of boxes of still-kicking Blue crabs for $0.79 a pound.
2. Kill and clean the crab. Place it, top shell down, on a heat-resistant plate. Scatter the onion, ginger, and wine on top of the crab.
3. Put the crab and plate into a preheated steamer. Steam over high heat for 15 minutes. Remove and cool.
4. Disjoint and crack the crab and arrange on a platter. Dip the meat into the dipping sauce and eat with green onions and ginger. Use the tip of the leg to dig out the meat.
5. Serves 2 as a light meal.
Ginger, Green Onion and Vinegar Dipping Sauce
1. Scatter green onions in a shallow saucer.
2. Top with the shredded ginger. Sprinkle sugar and salt evenly over the ginger.
3. Pour the vinegar over the ginger.
4. Heat the oil until it's hot but not smoking, and pour it over the mixture. It should sizzle lightly. If it is too hot, let it cool.
5. Makes about 2/3 cup.
